generated_oml:
instance ExternalRoomTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer monitoring external room temperature with ±0.5°C accuracy in operational range"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmission
]

instance InternalBoxTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer monitoring internal box temperature with ±0.5°C accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmission
]

instance InternalBoxTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer monitoring internal box temperature with ±0.5°C accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmission
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalRoomTemperatureSensor, InternalBoxTemperatureSensor1, InternalBoxTemperatureSensor2
    base:desc "Temperature data transmitted via RabbitMQ AMQP protocol over local network"
]

instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Temperature readings from three DS18S20 sensors with ±0.5°C accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelA, ThermalModelB
]

instance ThermalModelA : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simplified thermal model with 2 parameters: C_air and G_box; assumes uniform temperature and no heatbed thermal inertia"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonScipyLibrary, RaspberryPiPlatform
]

instance ThermalModelB : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Detailed thermal model with 4 parameters: C_heater, C_air, G_heater, G_box; accounts for heatbed thermal storage"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonScipyLibrary, RaspberryPiPlatform
]

instance RabbitMQBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ version 3.x message broker for AMQP protocol communication between Raspberry Pi and controller"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance PythonScipyLibrary : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python library with Scipy optimization tools for non-linear least squares calibration of thermal models"
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCalibrationService
]

instance RaspberryPiPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi 4 Model B platform running low-level drivers and control software"
    DTDFVocab:enables TemperaturePredictionService, RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance PCBDesignTools : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source PCB design tools for MOSFET/optocoupler circuitry design"
    DTDFVocab:enables SystemDesignService
]

instance GitHubRepository : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source GitHub repository for storing code and specifications of the digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelDevelopmentService
]

instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Heatbed controlled via MOSFETs and optocouplers for safe actuation of high-current requirements (~4A)"
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Fan operating at 12V/24V for air circulation in thermal incubator system"
]

instance RaspberryPi4Controller : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi 4 Model B controller with GPIO, Wi-Fi, and Bluetooth capabilities for system control and data processing"
]

instance ThermalIncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Styrofoam insulated container (polystyrene, air volume ~0.03 m³) containing heatbed, fan, temperature sensors, and Raspberry Pi controller"
    base:contains HeatbedActuator, FanActuator, ExternalRoomTemperatureSensor, InternalBoxTemperatureSensor1, InternalBoxTemperatureSensor2, RaspberryPi4Controller
]

instance TemperaturePredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation-based temperature prediction using calibrated thermal models to forecast thermal behavior under current operating conditions"
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperaturePredictionInsight
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring of temperature data for safety checks and optimization of thermal regulation parameters"
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyCheckInsight, ControlDecisionAction
]

instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Calibration of thermal model parameters using non-linear least squares optimization via Python Scipy"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelCalibrationInsight
]

instance SystemDesignService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Design and validation of PCB circuitry for electrical isolation and safe actuation"
    DTDFVocab:provides PCBDesignInsight
]

instance ModelDevelopmentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Iterative development and version control of thermal models and digital twin specifications"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelVersioningInsight
]

instance TemperaturePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analysis of thermal behavior under current operating conditions to support decision-making"
]

instance SafetyCheckInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring to prevent overheating by triggering safety shutdowns"
]

instance ModelCalibrationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Minimized residual error between simulation and experimental data through non-linear least squares optimization"
]

instance PCBDesignInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation of PCB design for electrical isolation and safe actuation"
]

instance ModelVersioningInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Version control of model specifications and code in GitHub repository"
]

instance ControlDecisionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generating heatbed activation duration H and wait duration C in bang-bang control based on real-time temperature data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The controller sends actuation commands (e.g., heatbed on/off, fan speed control) via RabbitMQ to the low-level driver, which executes these commands through the PCB's MOSFETs and optocouplers. Commands are processed in response to temperature feedback to maintain setpoints."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "A centralized architecture where RabbitMQ server (hosted on Raspberry Pi) orchestrates communication between the low-level driver (hardware interaction), controller (bang-bang control logic), and simulation models. Information flows via RabbitMQ topics: sensor data to controller, control commands to driver, and model outputs for monitoring."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin is integrated during the 'as operated' phase of the physical system's lifecycle, as explicitly stated in the abstract: 'Once the system is deployed, the models used to build it should not be discarded. Instead, they should be integrated into a Digital Twin.'"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity validated through experimental tests of assumptions (e.g., uniform temperature via three-sensor placement experiments). Model B's accuracy was confirmed by comparing simulation results against real-world thermal inertia data. Uncertainty is addressed via parameter calibration minimizing residual error (J = Σ(y_i - f(x_i, θ))²)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ (AMQP protocol) over a local network hosted on Raspberry Pi. Physical components (sensors, actuators) connect via GPIO to Raspberry Pi, with RabbitMQ serving as the communication backbone between PT components and DT services."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "All DT components (RabbitMQ server, low-level driver, controller, simulation models) are deployed on a single Raspberry Pi 4 Model B, leveraging its built-in Wi-Fi/Bluetooth for connectivity and GPIO for hardware interfacing."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The low-level driver incorporates a safe protocol to maintain device safety during operation. Specific cybersecurity measures or remote operation safety protocols are not detailed, but the system includes hardware-level isolation (MOSFETs/optocouplers) to prevent electrical faults."
]
